name: David Spinozza
abstract: David Spinozza (born August 8, 1949)  is an American guitarist and producer. He worked with former Beatles Paul McCartney, Ringo Starr and John Lennon during the 1970s, and had a long collaboration with singer-songwriter James Taylor, producing Taylor's album Walking Man.

interviews.title: David Spinozza interviewed by Monk Rowe, New Hartford, NY, September 27, 2025

works.t: Don't Let Me Be Lonely Tonight
Don't Let Me Be Lonely Tonight

about_quotes.speaker: Lou Marini
about_quotes.evidence: But you know like David Spinozza has a solo on “The Right Place The Right Time” a guitar solo which guitar players all over the world have learned to play.
about_quotes.doc_title: Lou Marini interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, June 15, 2023

about_quotes.speaker: Gene Bertoncini
about_quotes.evidence: And the younger crew like David Spinozza and — oh the guys are going to hate me for forgetting — I worked a lot with a guy named Jay Berliner
about_quotes.doc_title: Gene Bertoncini interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 13, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Bernard Purdie
about_quotes.evidence: guitar players was ... David Spinozza ... I mean for me I could not have ever asked for anybody any better than those musicians.
about_quotes.doc_title: Bernard Purdie interviewed by Monk Rowe, West Orange, New Jersey, July 30, 2003
